name: Apple-Phyllo Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat a nonstick skillet with cooking spray and place over medium heat until hot. Add diced apples and sauté for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often.
2. Add cinnamon, nutmeg, and brown sugar; toss well and cook an additional 3 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool.
3. Coat a flat surface with cooking spray. Place one sheet of phyllo on the surface (keep remaining phyllo covered). Spray the phyllo with butter-flavored cooking spray.
4. Layer two more sheets of phyllo on the first sheet, coating each with cooking spray. Cut vertically into 5 equal strips; cut each strip in half to make 10 pieces. Repeat this process with the remaining phyllo sheets, spraying each sheet with cooking spray.
5. Spoon about 1 1/2 teaspoons of apple mixture at the base of each strip and roll up in a jelly-roll fashion. Place seam side down on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray.
6. Bake at 375°F for 30 to 35 minutes or until golden. Remove from oven to wire rack and let cool slightly.
7. Combine remaining ingredients in a small bowl and stir well. Drizzle about 1 1/2 teaspoons of glaze over each roll. Serve warm.
Yields: 30 appetizers (30 calories each)
